spec_match.rationale: Touches local-network-access, local-network from the chromestatus summary but skips cross-site, TLS, aoU251p705FbIQjDuGs, loopback-network — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "1. CSRF Attack (no LNA)"; "2. Blocked by LNA ✓"; "3. Legitimate opt-in" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5152728072060928 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.sc-btn:hover:not(.active)).

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on csrf-scenario
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Local network access restrictions introduces cross-site, TLS, aoU251p705FbIQjDuGs, loopback-network but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://wicg.github.io/local-network-access

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on csrf-scenario
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring csrf-scenario back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.sc-btn:hover:not(.active)). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: csrf-scenario covers the happy path of Local network access restrictions and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
